summary: Cleaned up Young Link/CLink fighter typing by deleting the local ftCLink/types.h shim and reusing the existing Link shared types from ftLink/types.h. ftCl_Init.c now uses ftLk_Fighter and ftLk_DatAttrs instead of generic Fighter plus a duplicate CLinkAttributes struct, and ftCl_Init_801492F4 was corrected from u32 to bool to match its boolean command-variable use.
what_changed: Removed the ftCLink-local types.h file, including ftCLink_FighterVars and the duplicate CLinkAttributes typedef.
Replaced the include of ftCLink/types.h in ftCl_Init.c with ftLink/types.h.
Changed local fighter pointers in ftCl_Init.c from Fighter* to ftLk_Fighter* where CLink code is using Link-family fighter fields such as fv.lk, cmd_vars, specialn_facing_dir, and dat_attrs.
Replaced CLinkAttributes* casts with ftLk_DatAttrs* for ext_attr and dat_attrs access.
Renamed a local attributes variable from attrs/temp_r4 to ea or da in several places, with one field access clarified from x54 to attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_end.
Updated ftCl_Init_801492F4 return type in both the C file and header from u32 to bool, returning false instead of 0 when no fighter/command flag is present.
Set fp->cmd_vars[1] to false in ftCl_Init_80149318 instead of integer 0.
Refactored some local declarations to narrower scope, notably in ftCl_Init_8014919C, ftCl_Init_801492C4, and ftCl_Init_801492F4.
smart_moves: Avoided maintaining a duplicate CLinkAttributes layout by reusing ftLk_DatAttrs, consistent with the deleted file's own todo that CLinkAttributes was really ftLk_DatAttrs.
Used the more specific ftLk_Fighter type for CLink code, making Link-family fighter vars and data attributes explicit instead of relying on generic Fighter casts.
Kept the public header synchronized with the implementation when changing ftCl_Init_801492F4 from u32 to bool.
Converted command-variable boolean intent into bool returns and false assignment, improving type documentation without changing the apparent control flow meaning.
Preserved matching-sensitive unused stack annotations and MUST_MATCH blocks while doing type cleanup, suggesting the cleanup was careful not to disturb known matching constraints.
decomp_lessons: If a character-specific types.h only duplicates a shared sibling character layout, prefer deleting the duplicate and including the shared type header, especially for clone characters such as CLink/Link.
A local struct with padding and a todo saying it is another known type is a strong candidate for consolidation once the shared type is available.
Use character-specific Fighter typedefs such as ftLk_Fighter when accessing character-specific fighter vars; this reduces generic casts and makes field provenance clearer.
When a function returns a command-variable flag, check whether bool is a better signature than u32 and update both the source and header together.
Type cleanups can also reveal better field names: replacing x54 with attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_end documents the semantic meaning of a DAT attribute while preserving the offset-based layout.
For matching-sensitive code, keep existing MUST_MATCH padding comments/blocks unless there is evidence they are no longer required.
naming_conventions: Use ftLk_Fighter for Link-family fighter instances used by CLink code.
Use ftLk_DatAttrs for CLink/Link DAT attributes instead of a local CLinkAttributes alias.
Prefer semantic DAT attribute names where known, as in attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_end replacing x54.
Short local names ea and da were used to distinguish ext_attr-derived attributes from dat_attrs-derived attributes.
Boolean command-variable state should be represented with bool, false, and true where the function semantics are flag-like.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff retained MUST_MATCH unused stack padding blocks in ftCl_Init_OnLoad, item pickup/drop handlers, and ftCl_Init_8014919C, indicating type cleanup was done without removing known stack-shape aids.
Declarations were moved into narrower scopes while maintaining apparent behavior; this can improve C clarity but should be checked against matching output when locals affect stack layout.

Changing u32 to bool for ftCl_Init_801492F4 is safe evidence-wise only because the implementation returns command flag semantics and false, but future similar changes should verify caller expectations and generated code.
follow_up_queries: Find other character-specific types.h files with todo comments saying they duplicate another character's DAT attrs.
Search for remaining CLinkAttributes references after pr-843.
Search for functions returning u32 but only returning command-variable boolean flags.
Compare ftCLink and ftLink DAT attribute layouts for additional shared-field naming opportunities.
Check other clone fighters for generic Fighter* usage where a character-specific fighter typedef exists.
